meaning: a few, a small number of
example: I have a couple of questions.

english: A lot of people… That's a real thing.
meaning: used to validate that something is a common experience
example: A lot of people feel anxious on Mondays. That's a real thing.

meaning: a single unit of media (article, video, post)
example: We need to create a piece of content for this campaign.

meaning: a creative or professional output
example: Every piece of work we produce should reflect our values.

english: A ton of / a bunch of
meaning: a large amount of
example: We have a ton of feedback to go through.

meaning: used to shift from joking to being sincere
example: In all seriousness, this is a critical issue.

meaning: from beginning to end, completely
example: I read the report all the way through.

meaning: a wide variety of preferences
example: The menu caters to all these different tastes.

meaning: used to refer collectively to multiple items just mentioned
example: All these things add up over time.

meaning: enables or gives us the ability to
example: This tool will allow us to do much more with less effort.

meaning: despite the fact that; even though
example: Although it was difficult, we finished on time.

meaning: has consistently focused on
example: Our strategy has always been centered on the customer.

english: And more than anything, you have to be tough-minded
meaning: above all else, mental resilience is essential

english: And so, like [name] mentioned, we're really doing things a little bit differently this year.
meaning: referencing a colleague's point to introduce a change in approach

english: And then you have to be like
meaning: casual transition to describe a reaction or next step
example: And then you have to be like, okay, let's try again.

meaning: used to introduce a new, noteworthy point
example: Another interesting thing is that sales went up despite the slowdown.

meaning: asking for practical advice
example: Any tips on managing a remote team?

meaning: expressing doubt or asking for confirmation
example: Are you sure about that? I heard something different.

meaning: considering everything together; overall
example: As a whole, the campaign performed well.

meaning: to the best of my knowledge
example: As far as I know, the meeting is still on.

meaning: provided that; on the condition that
example: As long as we stay focused, we'll meet the deadline.

meaning: as I develop or my circumstances expand
example: As my life is growing more complex, I need better systems.

meaning: referring back to something already said
example: As previously mentioned, the deadline is Friday.

meaning: while we are investigating or exploring
example: As we are looking into new markets, we see a strong opportunity.

meaning: as is obvious or apparent
example: As you can tell, I'm very passionate about this topic.

meaning: in relation to what you're discussing
example: As you talk about the strategy, keep the budget in mind.

meaning: taking for granted that someone agrees or is comfortable
example: Assuming that everyone is okay, let's move on.

meaning: however; on the other hand
example: It's a great idea. At the same time though, the timing is tricky.

meaning: in the past; in earlier times
example: Back in the day, we used to do everything manually.

meaning: an unfair or undeserved negative reputation
example: Remote work gets a bad rap, but it works well for us.

meaning: to complement or offset each other
example: Their strengths and weaknesses balance each other out perfectly.

meaning: light, playful conversation or teasing
example: There was a lot of banter between the two presenters.

meaning: a casual way to explain a situation simply
example: Basically what's happening is demand has outpaced supply.

meaning: to be charged with or blamed for something
example: He was accused of misrepresenting the data.

meaning: to have the capability or opportunity to
example: I want to be able to present this clearly.

meaning: emphasizing that something is genuinely true for the speaker
example: I care about this project — because I do.

english: Before going into details, it's worth pointing out that
meaning: flagging something important before diving deeper

english: Being a public figure on the internet
meaning: referring to the experience of being publicly visible online
used to introduce something surprising
example: Believe it or not, this was done with no budget.

meaning: not being completely honest; exaggerating or misleading
example: I think he was bending the truth in that interview.

english: Bridge the gap / connect the dots / build the bridge
meaning: to link two things or resolve a gap
example: Our job is to bridge the gap between strategy and execution.

meaning: a category or group
example: Let's put these ideas into two buckets.

meaning: to enhance or boost creative thinking
example: Changing your environment can really bump up your creativity.

meaning: used to assert a personal view after acknowledging another side
example: But I do think that consistency matters most.

english: But I was making it even more high pressure
meaning: I was adding unnecessary stress to the situation

english: But the beautiful thing about it is
meaning: highlighting a positive or redeeming quality
example: But the beautiful thing about it is how simple it is.

meaning: getting to the main idea
example: But the point is, we need to decide by Friday.

meaning: we need to remain modest and open-minded
example: We've had success, but we gotta be humble about what we don't know.

english: But what I love to do is
meaning: introducing a personal preference or habit
example: But what I love to do is start with the customer perspective.

meaning: requesting a brief pause or focused discussion
example: Can we focus on the data for a minute?

english: Can you talk a little bit more about…?
meaning: asking someone to elaborate
example: Can you talk a little bit more about the budget breakdown?

meaning: resulted from or originated from
example: This idea came out of a late-night brainstorm.

meaning: to question or push back against accepted standards
example: Great creative always challenges those norms.

meaning: slightly irreverent or playfully bold
example: That's a cheeky headline — I love it.

meaning: to return to a topic later
example: Let's circle back to the budget question after the break.

meaning: an overused phrase or idea that has lost originality
example: Try to avoid clichés in the copy.

english: Come across the exact same challenge
meaning: to encounter the same problem
example: I've come across the exact same challenge with other clients.

meaning: to be useful at a particular time
example: That extra budget will definitely come in handy.

meaning: to become relevant or start to have an effect
example: That's where the data really comes into play.

meaning: a shared feature or characteristic
example: There's a strong commonality between these two audiences.

meaning: gathering information from multiple places
example: We're compiling all the different sources into one report.

english: Completely different from the one we had last year
meaning: a clear contrast with a previous version or situation

english: Constantly reframe it in a positive way
meaning: to keep shifting perspective toward the positive
example: When facing setbacks, constantly reframe it in a positive way.

meaning: the action of keeping something under control
example: Containment of the crisis was the first priority.

meaning: expressing a past possibility that didn't happen
example: This could have been avoided with better planning.

meaning: a chance to do something innovative
example: This brief is a real creative opportunity.

meaning: to break something down into its parts to understand it
example: Let's deconstruct the campaign to see what worked.

meaning: the process of analyzing something by breaking it apart
example: A full deconstruction of the strategy revealed key gaps.

meaning: to put something into action or distribute it
example: We'll deploy the new system next quarter.

meaning: hard to identify or notice
example: The shift was subtle and difficult to recognize at first.

meaning: to take apart or systematically disassemble
example: We need to dismantle the old process before building the new one.

meaning: to analyze something carefully and in detail
example: Let's dissect the brief before we respond.

meaning: to reduce to the essential elements
example: Can we distill this down to three core ideas?

meaning: to start exploring something in depth
example: Let's dive into the research findings.

meaning: to use any means necessary
example: We'll do whatever it takes to hit the deadline.

english: Do you know what I'm saying? / Does it make sense?
meaning: checking for understanding or agreement
example: It's about the long game, you know what I'm saying?

meaning: used to clarify that you don't mean to offend
example: Don't get me wrong, the work is good — I just think we can push further.

meaning: don't get confused or misunderstand
example: Don't get twisted — this isn't about blame.

english: Double down on our marketing effort
meaning: to increase commitment or investment in something
example: Given the momentum, let's double down on our marketing effort.

meaning: relaxed and not easily upset
example: She's very easy going — a pleasure to work with.

meaning: it's not difficult to make mistakes
example: With such a tight timeline, it's easy to screw things up.

meaning: financial analysis forms the foundation of this
to raise the level or quality from one state to another
example: We want to elevate from a product message to a brand story.

meaning: to fully represent or express an emotion
example: The visual should embody a feeling of freedom.

meaning: the mental/emotional capacity to deal with something
example: After that meeting, I had no emotional bandwidth left.

meaning: to understand and share the feelings of another based on shared experience
example: I can empathize — I went through the same thing.

meaning: to face or come across circumstances
example: You'll encounter situations where there's no clear answer.

meaning: very large; huge
example: The response to the campaign was enormous.

meaning: from a particular point in time until now
example: Ever since the rebrand, engagement has been up.

meaning: occasionally; from time to time
example: Every now and then you need to step back and reassess.

meaning: each individual output or project
example: Every piece of work should have a clear idea behind it.

meaning: used to confirm agreement or precision
example: That's exactly what I was thinking.

meaning: visually striking or astonishing
example: The numbers were eye-popping — 200% growth in one quarter.

meaning: expressing acceptance of a point or argument
example: You want more time to review it? Fair enough.

meaning: extremely interesting
example: That's a fascinating approach — tell me more.

meaning: a few weeks ago
example: We discussed this a few weeks back in the planning session.

meaning: to make small adjustments for improvement
example: Just fine-tune the wording and it'll be perfect.

english: Finish line holds you back from your potential
meaning: focusing only on the end goal can limit growth along the way

english: For me, anyway / For me, it is
meaning: expressing a personal perspective without imposing it
example: Consistency is what matters most — for me, anyway.

english: For me, the fluency is being able to
meaning: defining what fluency personally means
example: For me, the fluency is being able to think in the language.

meaning: seriously; genuinely
example: For real, that presentation was impressive.

meaning: certainly; definitely
example: For sure, we can get that done by Thursday.

english: For the benefit of podcast listeners
meaning: providing context for those who are only listening, not seeing
example: For the benefit of podcast listeners, I'm looking at a chart right now.

meaning: no matter how hard I try
example: I can't, for the life of me, remember where I saved that file.

meaning: mostly; in general
example: For the most part, the feedback has been positive.

meaning: to continue boldly despite difficulties
example: Despite the setbacks, we decided to forge ahead.

meaning: to frighten or make someone feel very anxious
example: Public speaking used to really freak me out.

meaning: used to introduce something ironic or unexpected
example: Funny thing is, I was just thinking the same thing.

meaning: to escape blame or consequence
example: You can't get away with sloppy work in this industry.

meaning: to get to the specific, practical details
example: Let's stop theorizing and get down to the nitty-gritty.

meaning: to misunderstand or confuse something
example: Sorry, I got my wires crossed — I thought the meeting was Thursday.

meaning: expression of disbelief or surprise
example: You finished the whole report in one night? Get outta here!

meaning: to acknowledge someone's contribution
example: I want to give credit to the whole team on this one.

meaning: to try as hard as you can
example: We might not win, but give it your best shot.

meaning: to create an impression of
example: The data gives us the sense that demand is shifting.

english: Given all of that / given all of the above
meaning: considering everything mentioned
example: Given all of that, I think we should proceed carefully.

english: Go and get your mom / Come and see my new car
meaning: directional phrases combining two verbs of motion
example: Go and get the files from the printer.

meaning: informal praise or approval
example: Good stuff — this is exactly what we needed.

meaning: with skepticism; not taking something too literally
example: Take that forecast with a grain of salt.

meaning: to stop completely after slowing down
example: Production ground to a halt during the outage.

meaning: at the midpoint of something
example: We're halfway through the project and already over budget.

meaning: obstructing or slowing down development
example: Bureaucracy is hampering growth across the industry.

meaning: a small number of
example: Only a handful of clients responded to the survey.

english: Having said that / That said / That being said
meaning: introducing a contrasting point
example: The results are strong. That said, there's room to improve.

english: Having worked with [organization] and going back and forth with them
meaning: describing an ongoing collaborative or negotiation process
to go towards
example: We're headed over to the client office at 3pm.

meaning: the core essence or values of a brand
example: Authenticity is at the heart of this brand.

meaning: introducing a recommendation
example: Here's some advice for you — always over-prepare for pitches.

meaning: introducing an important point, often a complication or nuance
example: Here's the thing — the timeline doesn't work with the budget.

meaning: based on past trends or patterns
example: Historically, Q4 is our strongest quarter.

meaning: to be personally relevant or emotionally affecting
example: That story really hit close to home.

meaning: wait briefly; pause
example: Hold on a second — I want to make sure I understand.

meaning: asking for the group's opinion or reaction
example: How do we feel about launching in March?

english: How do we take advantage of this
meaning: how can we leverage this opportunity
example: The market is shifting — how do we take advantage of this?

meaning: asking someone to describe their vision or idea
example: How do you envision the final product?

meaning: attempting to say "to what extent."
example: To what extent will this affect the rollout?

meaning: the quality of being modest and respectful
example: Great leaders lead with humility.

meaning: a strong personal endorsement
example: I absolutely recommend visiting the new exhibition.

english: I am amazed / humbled by everything you do
meaning: expressing deep appreciation for someone's efforts

english: I am a big fan of
meaning: expressing strong admiration for something
example: I am a big fan of the iterative approach.

meaning: a positive response to "how are you?"

english: I am more than happy to
meaning: very willing to do something
example: I am more than happy to walk you through the proposal.

english: I can't get it out of my head
meaning: unable to stop thinking about something
example: That melody — I can't get it out of my head.

meaning: I find that intolerable
example: I cannot bear that kind of dishonesty in a team.

meaning: reflecting on a missed or alternative response
example: Looking back, I could have said it more diplomatically.

english: I do appreciate the fact that
meaning: expressing genuine gratitude
example: I do appreciate the fact that you flagged this early.

english: I do have a tendency to say yes to everything
meaning: acknowledging a habit of over-committing
confirming a recollection
example: I do remember discussing this in the last meeting.

meaning: expressing genuine confusion about one's own behavior
example: I just kept checking the numbers. I don't even know why.

english: I don't know how to say this
meaning: struggling to find the right words for something difficult

english: I don't wanna skip it because everyone is interested
meaning: choosing to cover something despite time pressure
expressing a personal impression or intuition
example: I feel like we're missing a key insight here.

meaning: expressing a positive emotional state

english: I found that this has to be true in my life
meaning: a personal realization or discovery

english: I gotta be honest with you
meaning: introducing a frank or candid statement
example: I gotta be honest with you — the brief is unclear.

meaning: having goals or ambitions
example: I aspire to lead a team that does work that matters.

english: I have been obsessed with… ever since I was a little kid
meaning: describing a lifelong passion or interest

english: I just got it quite recently
meaning: acquired something not long ago
example: I just got it quite recently but already love it.

english: I just kinda jumped in there
meaning: acted spontaneously without overthinking
example: I wasn't sure if it was my place, but I just kinda jumped in there.

english: I just want to make sure to
meaning: emphasizing care or diligence
example: I just want to make sure to cover all the key points.

meaning: wanting to advance to the next slide
expressing total agreement or understanding
example: I know exactly what you mean.

english: I know he'll say no / I know you're wrong
meaning: expressing confident disagreement or anticipation of rejection
referencing something discovered about someone
example: I read earlier that you launched this in record time.

meaning: expressing a moment of understanding
example: I realized that I'd been approaching it the wrong way.

english: I remember one time I actually booked a hotel there, not knowing it had a bad reputation, and coming back at midnight is not the safest environment to be walking.
meaning: Longer anecdote; no explanation needed

english: I think the build-up in [song/section] is very much like a six-stroke roll
meaning: a musician describing rhythmic technique in a specific moment

english: I thought back when I started the business
meaning: reflecting on early assumptions or beliefs
expressing complete agreement
example: I totally agree — the approach needs to change.

meaning: indicating what you intended to say before being interrupted
example: I was gonna say, we should have flagged this earlier.

english: I was putting myself in this high-pressure situation
meaning: describing self-imposed stress
describing an emotional reaction
example: I was really like, I can't believe this worked.

english: I was so upset, so messed up
meaning: expressing a state of emotional distress

english: I wouldn't put it past him
meaning: it's not surprising or unlikely given what I know about them
example: I wouldn't put it past him to pull out last minute.

english: I'll be able to introduce you to each team
meaning: offering to facilitate introductions
agreeing to join someone at a location
expressing interest in learning more
example: I'm curious about how you approached the research.

meaning: used to emphasize sincerity or certainty
example: I'm telling you, this is the best brief we've had all year.

english: I'm very impressed you did learn a fair amount of Japanese for this role
meaning: expressing genuine admiration for someone's language effort

english: If I give you one [thing], that would be…
meaning: distilling advice or insight to a single point
example: If I give you one piece of advice, that would be: stay curious.

english: If I look at some of the examples around
meaning: using specific cases to support a point
example: If I look at some of the examples around sustainability, we see a clear pattern.

meaning: checking comprehension before responding
example: If I understand this correctly, you're proposing a two-phase launch?

english: If I was in that situation
meaning: considering a hypothetical
example: If I was in that situation, I'd ask for more time.

meaning: checking that the listener follows
example: It's a layered approach — if that makes sense.

english: If you are digging down with whys
meaning: if you're asking deeper questions to find root causes

english: If you are referring to the situation…
meaning: clarifying which specific situation is being discussed

english: If you could do this, that would be great
meaning: a polite request
example: If you could send the deck by Friday, that would be great.

meaning: stating a consequence of inaction
example: If you don't address it now, it becomes a bigger problem.

meaning: something understood only by those who've experienced it
example: That creative pressure right before a pitch — if you know, you know.

meaning: used to introduce a slightly unusual or figurative expression
example: It's a creative reset, if you will.

english: In another word / In other words
meaning: rephrasing something more clearly
example: In other words, the strategy didn't work.

meaning: related to; in relation to
example: In connection with last week's discussion, here's the update.

meaning: in a very difficult or serious situation
example: Without a backup plan, we're in deep.

meaning: regardless of which option is chosen
example: In either way, we need to be ready by Monday.

english: In order to be / In order to do
meaning: expressing purpose or requirement
example: In order to be effective, we need clearer goals.

meaning: to some degree; not entirely
example: The delay was partly due to supply chain issues.

meaning: especially; specifically
example: I want to highlight one slide in particular.

meaning: in a specific domain or field
example: We have strong expertise in this particular area.

meaning: to begin with; originally
example: Why did we agree to this timeline in the first place?

meaning: as; presented as
example: The feedback came in the form of a detailed report.

english: In the last couple of years
meaning: over the recent past
example: In the last couple of years, we've doubled our output.

english: In the level of the industry at large
meaning: across the broader industry
example: This is a challenge at the level of the industry at large.

meaning: explained in the most basic way
example: At the simplest level, it's about trust.

english: In this day and age / these days
meaning: in the current era
example: In this day and age, digital-first is the default.

meaning: to a great degree; extremely
example: The campaign performed incredibly well.

meaning: as a basic or essential characteristic
example: This approach is inherently more scalable.

english: Intersection of brand contribution and cultural tension
meaning: the point where a brand's role and a cultural conflict or debate meet
checking whether a summary or statement is accurate
example: So the core issue is alignment — is that fair to say?

meaning: asking for clarification on whether something implies a conclusion
example: Is that to say we're changing direction?

meaning: it's too important to ignore
example: The data on retention can't be overlooked.

english: It doesn't have to be huge
meaning: small actions or changes can also be valid
example: It doesn't have to be huge — even a small gesture matters.

meaning: something that triggered deeper reflection
example: That conversation really got me thinking about our process.

meaning: expressing strong agreement with something
example: It is so true — consistency really is everything.

english: It isn't just a car company
meaning: reframing a brand's identity beyond its obvious category
it fits perfectly across the board
example: The tone just matches everything we've been building.

meaning: it is completely logical or reasonable
example: The pivot makes tons of sense given the data.

meaning: this is not an obligation or default
example: It shouldn't necessarily be the most expensive option.

english: It sounds like we are very aligned
meaning: we appear to be in agreement
example: It sounds like we are very aligned on priorities.

meaning: simple expression of a positive experience
example: It was great — the energy in the room was fantastic.

meaning: it made a lasting impression
example: The opening of the presentation was really memorable.

english: It was so bizarre / It's so bizarre
meaning: it was very strange or unusual
example: The feedback was so bizarre — completely out of left field.

english: It worth pointing out before going into details
meaning: flagging something important before diving deeper
it's a lot of fun
example: Working with this team is a blast.

meaning: the central focus or key factor is
example: At the end of the day, it's all about the audience.

meaning: multiple factors are involved
example: The success isn't one thing — it's a combination of things.

meaning: expressing appreciation or excitement about something
example: It's cool to see that the client is already using it.

english: It's going to knock you out
meaning: it will be an overwhelming or amazing experience
example: Wait until you see the final cut — it's going to knock you out.

english: It's got a couple of onboard effects
meaning: the device has built-in features or functions
it's surprising or hard to believe
example: It's kinda crazy how fast things moved.

english: It's like we're meant to be
meaning: it feels naturally fated or perfectly suited
example: This collaboration — it's like we're meant to be.

english: It's maybe a whole different thing but
meaning: introducing a somewhat tangential point
example: It's maybe a whole different thing, but this reminds me of a past project.

english: It's not about you. It's about the team
meaning: putting collective goals above personal ones

english: It's not binary — it's a bit of both
meaning: the answer isn't simply one or the other
example: Is it a brand problem or a product problem? It's not binary — it's a bit of both.

meaning: correcting a framing and offering a better one
example: It's not about speed — it's more about reliability.

meaning: it's a higher-order concept or layer
example: The strategy is one level up from the execution.

meaning: it's generally good quality
example: The draft is pretty solid overall — just a few edits needed.

meaning: there is no risk involved
example: You can say that in the pitch — it's safe, completely safe.

english: It's simple and straightforward but it allows us to do…
meaning: despite its simplicity, it enables something significant
it's excellent, impressive
example: That concept is so fire — let's go with it.

english: It's worth pointing out before going into details
meaning: flagging something important before diving deeper
to repeat a process with improvements each time
example: We'll iterate on the design based on user feedback.

meaning: introducing an anecdote from personal experience

english: Just a quick build on that
meaning: adding briefly to a previous point
example: Just a quick build on that — we should also consider mobile users.

english: Just to give listeners a sense of scale
meaning: providing context for the magnitude of something
establishing a shared baseline or context
example: Just to ground the conversation, here's where we started.

english: Just to set the stage for everybody
meaning: providing background before getting into detail
example: Just to set the stage for everybody, here's the situation we're in.

meaning: to monitor closely
example: Keep an eye on the engagement numbers this week.

meaning: to prevent or stop from doing something
example: We need to keep the team from burning out before launch.

english: Keep the best version of the organization
meaning: maintaining high standards and culture as things evolve
highly talented and effective creative leaders
the broader environment or competitive field
example: The competitive landscape has shifted significantly.

meaning: to draw on psychological insights about consumers
example: The campaign really leans into consumer psychology.

meaning: asking to clarify or confirm understanding
example: Let me get this straight — we're relaunching in Q2?

meaning: requesting time to examine something
example: Let me look at it before I give you my opinion.

meaning: used to emphasize what's coming next
example: Let me tell you — that pitch was something else.

meaning: calling for candor
example: Let's be honest — the timeline was always too tight.

meaning: wanting to complete something unpleasant quickly
example: Let's get this over with so we can move on.

english: Let's just be clear on that point
meaning: emphasizing a specific point to avoid confusion
example: Let's just be clear — this is a pilot, not a full launch.

meaning: starting with something specific
example: Let's kick things off with a quick recap of last quarter.

meaning: to use something to maximum advantage
example: We can leverage the existing audience to grow faster.

meaning: people who share similar values or thinking
example: The conference brought together like-minded people from across the industry.

meaning: asking for specific examples
example: The campaign had a unique element." — "Like what?

meaning: referencing something someone else stated
example: Like you said, the fundamentals are strong.

english: List it ride / Let it ride
meaning: to leave something as is; not interfere
example: Let's not over-edit this — let it ride.

meaning: used for emphasis (often not literal)
example: I literally cannot believe the results.

meaning: summarizing briefly after detail
example: Long story short, we won the pitch.

meaning: to have a significant impact
example: Even small changes in copy can make a big difference.

meaning: to manage with what's available
example: We'll have to make do with the current budget.

meaning: it may apply or work for certain people
example: This approach might not work for everyone, but it does make sense for some people.

meaning: to be proactive and effective
example: We need people who can make things happen under pressure.

meaning: to plan or outline in detail
example: Let's map out the next three months.

english: Marketing is pretty much driven by product innovation
meaning: product development is the main source of marketing direction

english: May be making a stupid question
meaning: self-deprecating way to introduce a question
example: Maybe making a stupid question, but — why this market first?

meaning: clearing one's mind of unnecessary thoughts
example: Take a break for some mental declutter before the afternoon session.

english: Mix of different creative / mix of feelings / performances
meaning: a combination or blend of elements
example: We're looking for a mix of different creative approaches.

meaning: to a greater degree than before
example: Now more than ever, storytelling matters.

meaning: the primary or most significant function
example: The most important role of the creative is to build trust.

meaning: probably; in all probability
example: Most likely we'll go with option B.

meaning: quoting or referencing what a creative director told the speaker

english: My second piece of advice is
meaning: offering a second recommendation
example: My second piece of advice is to always test before you scale.

english: My younger self / future self / my authentic self
meaning: reflecting on different versions of oneself over time or in aspiration
unpleasant, shocking, or impressively intense
example: That was a nasty turn of events.

meaning: a word or phrase that carries an unfavorable association
example: The word 'cheap' has a negative connotation we want to avoid.

meaning: the specific, practical details
example: Now let's get into the nitty-gritty of execution.

english: No matter how hard you try
meaning: regardless of effort
example: No matter how hard you try, some things are outside your control.

english: No one wants to see the finished article
meaning: people are interested in the process and journey, not just the end result

english: No questions! / No question about it
meaning: expressing absolute certainty
example: She's the right person for the role — no question.

meaning: without any logic or pattern
example: The edits came back with no rhyme or reason.

meaning: a guiding principle or ultimate goal
example: Keeping the audience at the center is our north star.

meaning: not always; this is not a definite conclusion
example: A bigger budget doesn't necessarily mean better results.

english: Now I can't remember what your question was
meaning: acknowledging a lapse in memory mid-conversation
small but valuable insights
example: The interview was full of nuggets of wisdom.

meaning: several methods or approaches
example: We can address this in a number of different ways.

meaning: expression of surprise, concern, or dismay
example: Oh boy — that's a much bigger issue than I thought.

english: Oh well / It can't be helped
meaning: accepting something unfortunate that can't be changed
example: Oh well — we'll just have to adjust the plan.

english: On one hand… on the other hand
meaning: presenting two contrasting perspectives
example: On one hand, the data is promising. On the other hand, the timeline is tight.

english: On the cryptocurrency side of things
meaning: regarding cryptocurrency
example: On the crypto side of things, there's been a lot of volatility.

meaning: from immediate memory without checking
example: Off the top of my head, I'd say we have about 30 contacts.

english: Once we get into the meeting
meaning: when we're in the meeting
example: Once we get into the meeting, let me do the talking first.

meaning: extremely rare or exceptional
example: That kind of creative talent is one in a million.

english: One of the big things we've been talking about
meaning: a major recurring topic
example: One of the big things we've been talking about is sustainability.

meaning: identifying a specific context or area
example: One of the places where we can really improve is onboarding.

english: One of the things we took seriously is
meaning: emphasizing a priority
example: One of the things we took seriously is making it accessible.

meaning: referring to a recognizable or common situation
example: It's one of those things that's obvious in hindsight.

english: One thing I was thinking is that
meaning: introducing a personal idea or observation
example: One thing I was thinking is that we start smaller.

meaning: on a regular, everyday level
example: On a day-to-day basis, the tool saves us hours.

meaning: already existing as the default
example: The system originally in place wasn't designed for this scale.

english: Our focus is very much about
meaning: clearly stating the central priority
example: Our focus is very much about sustainable growth.

meaning: existing in the world; or unconventional
example: There's a lot of great work out there to learn from.

meaning: something significantly different from the norm
example: That quarter was an outlier — not indicative of the trend.

meaning: encompassing everything; at the highest level
example: The overarching strategy is to be the category leader.

meaning: to reverse or invalidate
example: The new research overturned our assumptions.

meaning: the second proposal is a rough guide, not final
contributing factor is
example: Part of that is due to how we've been positioning the product.

meaning: routes or methods toward a goal
example: There are multiple pathways to achieving this.

english: Peace of work / piece of work
meaning: a completed item of creative or professional output
example: Every piece of work should have a clear strategic foundation.

english: People are doing that! That's a real thing.
meaning: validating that a behavior or trend genuinely exists
to spread through something gradually
example: We want the brand idea to permeate every touchpoint.

meaning: to unfairly categorize or limit someone
example: Don't pigeonhole him as just a data person — he's a strong storyteller.

english: Pitching an idea, getting shut down, pitching again
meaning: describing the iterative, resilient nature of the creative process
to be critically important
example: Research plays a pivotal role in how we develop our ideas.

meaning: inviting the listener to ask questions or interject

english: Plus one to whatever [name] just said
meaning: expressing complete agreement with a previous speaker
the general conditions of a given environment
example: The social climate right now calls for a more empathetic approach.

meaning: mainly; for the most part
example: The audience is predominantly under 35.

meaning: excessively moralistic or lecturing in tone
example: We don't want the message to come across as preachy.

meaning: prices increased dramatically and quickly
example: After the announcement, the price skyrocketed overnight.

meaning: how much we've advanced toward our key performance indicators
to challenge thinking and motivate action
example: Great creative should both provoke and inspire.

meaning: direct, impactful, and concise
example: Make the headline more punchy.

meaning: to remove completely; to get rid of
example: Let's purge out the outdated data before the presentation.

meaning: to be completely open and transparent
example: In the pitch, just put it all out there.

meaning: to remove or displace from a position or situation
example: Automation didn't put me out of a job — it changed it.

english: Put together a book / put together
meaning: to compile or assemble
example: We put together a deck that covered the whole year.

meaning: honestly and directly
example: Quite frankly, the brief wasn't clear enough.

meaning: deeply meaningful or insightful
example: That observation is quite profound.

meaning: expressing significant worry
example: The report is raising serious concerns around data privacy.

english: Rather than thinking that creativity is the opposite of performance marketing
meaning: reframing creativity and performance as complementary, not opposing
to reconnect with who you truly are
example: After burnout, taking time to recover your authentic self is essential.

english: Regardless of segment they are in
meaning: no matter which category they belong to
example: This applies to all consumers, regardless of segment.

meaning: connects or applies to
example: This relates to what we discussed about brand purpose.

meaning: to refill or restore
example: We need to replenish our creative energy before the next sprint.

meaning: confirming the central insight
example: Consistency — right, that's the key!

meaning: approximately
example: It'll take roughly two weeks to complete.

meaning: an extreme or exaggerated experience
example: That feedback session sent me to another dimension.

meaning: the feeling of being part of a group
example: The campaign taps into a deep sense of belonging.

meaning: fixed and unchangeable
example: The deadline isn't set in stone — let's discuss.

english: Shopping just for the sake of it
meaning: buying without a specific need or purpose

english: So special — we couldn't be more excited
meaning: expressing great enthusiasm about something
introducing the reasoning behind a plan
used to indicate a figurative or approximate expression
example: We're flying blind, so to speak.

meaning: to share and get buy-in from leadership informally before a formal presentation

english: Something like that / somewhere around that
meaning: approximately or roughly that
example: Budget-wise, it's somewhere around that figure.

meaning: something the relevant people already know
example: The delay is something they are already aware of.

english: Sooner or later / sooner than later
meaning: eventually; ideally before too much time passes
example: We need to address this sooner than later.

meaning: a catalyst or initial inspiration
example: That conversation was the spark for the whole campaign.

meaning: transitioning to a related topic
example: Speaking of deadlines, have we confirmed the review date?

english: Standing on the shoulders of giants
meaning: building on the work and knowledge of those who came before
to begin
example: Let's start off with a quick check-in.

meaning: to begin from scratch
example: We might have to start over if the brief changes.

meaning: to avoid involvement in
example: Let's stay out of that debate for now.

meaning: to cook quickly over high heat while stirring. Also used metaphorically to mean mixing things rapidly
example: We're kind of stir-frying ideas right now.

meaning: to stop delaying and take action
example: Stop procrastinating and just send the draft.

meaning: without being fully aware
example: I subconsciously avoided the harder question.

meaning: expressing deep appreciation for an opportunity
example: It's such an honor to be presenting here today.

meaning: a particularly powerful creative instinct or vision
what was expected or intended
example: This was supposed to be a five-minute call.

meaning: to feel sorry for someone's situation, even without shared experience
example: I sympathize — it sounds like a really difficult position.

meaning: to access or make use of
example: We need to tap into a younger demographic.

english: That being said / Having said that / That said
meaning: introducing a contrasting point after acknowledging something
example: The results are good. That being said, we can do better.

meaning: that might be the reason or answer
example: Maybe it was the headline — that could be it.

meaning: acknowledging something important that must be stated
example: That has to be said, even if it's uncomfortable.

english: That is none of your business
meaning: it's a private matter
example: What I earn is none of your business.

english: That is pretty much the dream job
meaning: it closely matches one's ideal role

english: That is very, very early stages
meaning: it's still in the beginning phase
example: That is very, very early stages — nothing is confirmed.

english: That makes so much sense to me
meaning: expressing strong agreement with an explanation
example: Oh, that makes so much sense to me now.

english: That said, we're not seeing basic themes
meaning: despite the context, we're seeing something more sophisticated
acknowledging an enjoyable aspect of the work
two things that work exceptionally well together
example: Creativity and data? That's a killer combination.

meaning: much of what people believe is incorrect
example: There's a lot of myth around how this technology works.

meaning: expressing genuine interest after a conversation or presentation
describing an informal or approximate approach
it happens frequently; not unusual
example: That's pretty common in early-stage companies.

meaning: expressing mild to genuine interest
example: That's pretty interesting — I hadn't considered that angle.

meaning: it's a core, essential point
example: That's so fundamental — we need to get it right.

meaning: introducing a personal connection to a topic

english: That's the most important thing today
meaning: emphasizing the priority of a specific point
explaining the reason
example: The brief was vague — that's why we missed the mark.

meaning: explaining the rationale or concept
example: The idea behind that is to reduce friction for the user.

meaning: adding a new dimension to a topic
example: The other thing about this market is the speed of change.

english: The other thing that we want to be able to do
meaning: adding a secondary objective
example: The other thing we want to be able to do is track engagement over time.

meaning: the reverse; the opposite
example: It's not the product driving the brand — it's the other way around.

meaning: speaking about the advertising industry broadly
in terms of theme or subject matter
example: Thematically, all three concepts are aligned.

meaning: introducing a traditional piece of wisdom

english: There is a kind of similarity between what you do and what I do
meaning: drawing a parallel between two roles or approaches

english: There is a couple of things going on there
meaning: multiple factors are at play
example: There are a couple of things going on there that we need to unpack.

english: There were two things: one was… the other was…
meaning: structuring a response around two key points
expressing satisfaction or confirmation
example: There you go — that's exactly the insight we needed.

english: There's no need for you to know
meaning: the information is not relevant or accessible to you
there is a common pattern or inclination
example: There's the tendency to over-complicate things when the budget is tight.

english: This actually relates to today's topic
meaning: connecting a digression back to the main subject

english: This has broader implications in the sense that
meaning: this matters beyond the immediate context
example: This has broader implications in the sense that it could reshape the category.

meaning: this is a lot of fun
example: Working on this project is a real blast.

english: This is gonna be real quick background info
meaning: providing a brief context before the main point

english: This is just a small, subtle thing
meaning: a minor but notable detail
example: This is just a small, subtle thing but it changes the whole tone.

meaning: this is a rough, exploratory exercise

english: This is pretty much the same thing
meaning: these two things are essentially equivalent
this will be brief
example: This is real quick — I just need your sign-off.

meaning: expressing frustration
example: This is so annoying — we've explained it three times already.

meaning: a declaration of identity or values
example: Honest, direct, human — this is who we are.

meaning: on this occasion, as opposed to previous ones
example: This time around, let's start with the audience insight.

meaning: pointing to or referencing something in a presentation or diagram
considering or evaluating with sustainability as the frame
example: We're approaching every brief through the lens of sustainability.

meaning: to connect back to a main idea or goal
example: Every execution should tie back to the core strategy.

english: To be eligible to be creative effective
meaning: to qualify for creative effectiveness recognition (e.g., awards)

english: To me, this is pretty much a big deal
meaning: personally, this matters significantly
example: To me, this is pretty much a big deal — we shouldn't rush it.

meaning: a pioneer; someone who leads the way
example: She's a trailblazer in sustainable fashion marketing.

english: Trying to put together different things
meaning: attempting to integrate multiple elements
example: We're still trying to put together different things into one cohesive narrative.

meaning: to stop overthinking and relax or act spontaneously
example: Sometimes you just need to turn off your brain and write freely.

english: Two sides of the same coin
meaning: two different aspects of the same thing
example: Brand and performance are two sides of the same coin.

english: Two steps forward, one step back
meaning: making progress despite occasional setbacks

english: Two types of audiences you gotta be dealing with
meaning: identifying two distinct groups that must be addressed
absolutely not; in no situation
example: Under no circumstances should we share this externally.

meaning: supported or based on
example: The whole strategy is underpinned by consumer insight.

meaning: to reveal or activate the core creative approach
facing a significant obstacle
example: We're up against a real challenge with this timeline.

meaning: until this point in time
example: Up until now, we've been reactive rather than proactive.

meaning: specific scenarios in which something is applied
example: Let's walk through three use cases for this tool.

meaning: something concrete and real
example: The impact on morale is a very tangible thing.

meaning: to explain step by step
example: Can you walk us through the research findings?

english: We are down to the wire
meaning: we are very close to the deadline
example: We're down to the wire — all hands on deck.

english: We are in an active conversation about it
meaning: the discussion is ongoing
example: We are in an active conversation about the partnership.

english: We are more than happy to
meaning: very willing and glad to
example: We are more than happy to revise the proposal.

meaning: we are providing data or content to an audience
expressing a conviction or acknowledgment
example: We believe this is the right direction.

english: We bumped into some technical issues
meaning: we encountered unexpected technical problems

english: We do a lot of things
meaning: acknowledging a wide range of activities
example: We do a lot of things well, but focus is what we need now.

english: We don't have much time to work on
meaning: the available time for this is limited
describing a future state we aim for
example: We envision ourselves being the go-to agency for sustainability.

english: We have a couple of different ideas about how something like [X] could work
meaning: presenting several potential approaches

english: We know how to do those things
meaning: expressing confidence in existing capabilities

english: We see things a little bit differently
meaning: our perspective diverges from the norm
example: We see things a little bit differently — and that's our edge.

english: We will share some of the trends of
meaning: presenting key patterns in a given area

english: We'll figure it out / We'll figure it out together
meaning: expressing confidence that a solution will be found
example: It's unclear right now, but we'll figure it out together.

english: We're not seeing basic themes like [X]. Instead, we're seeing things like [Y].
meaning: describing a more sophisticated or nuanced trend

english: Well, if that sounds okay, I'm gonna share my screen
meaning: politely transitioning to a presentation

english: What I interpreted from this is
meaning: sharing one's understanding of something
example: What I interpreted from this is that the audience wants more depth.

english: What I mean by that is
meaning: clarifying a previous statement
example: What I mean by that is the message needs to be simpler.

english: What I really love about [brand] is
meaning: expressing genuine admiration for a brand quality

english: What is more [being said] here is
meaning: identifying the deeper or more important point
example: What is more being said here is that trust is eroding.

english: What is your sense in terms of… / Do you have the sense that…?
meaning: asking for an impression or gut feeling
example: What's your sense — will they approve it this quarter?

meaning: casual way to clarify or define something
example: What it is is a content gap in the mid-funnel.

meaning: highlighting a notable point
example: What's really interesting here is the shift in the under-25 demographic.

english: What really scares me is that
meaning: expressing a genuine concern
example: What really scares me is that we have no contingency plan.

meaning: describing the prior situation
example: What was happening before was we were reacting, not planning.

english: What we understand from these findings
meaning: summarizing the takeaway from data or research
asking for someone's opinion or assessment
example: What would you say is the biggest barrier to adoption?

meaning: what will set something apart
example: What's gonna differentiate us is the quality of the insight.

english: What's not to love about this
meaning: expressing that something is entirely likeable
example: It's bold, clear, and human. What's not to love?

meaning: expressing surprise at something impressive
example: What's stunning is how quickly they turned this around.

english: What's unique about [my show / our approach] is
meaning: identifying a distinctive quality
example: What's unique about our approach is the emphasis on earned media.

meaning: asking for the top insight or lesson from something

english: Whatever it is / whatever it may be
meaning: regardless of the specifics
example: Whatever it is, we need a solution by Thursday.

meaning: introducing what comes to mind about a topic
example: When I think of authentic storytelling, I think of this campaign.

meaning: regarding; on the topic of
example: When it comes to budget, we need to be realistic.

meaning: directing attention to data or a subject
example: When we look at the numbers, the trend is clear.

english: Whether… or… doesn't matter to me
meaning: expressing indifference between two options
noting that something is a separate, distinct matter
example: That's a which-is-different-thing situation — let's not conflate them.

meaning: discussing task assignment
example: Before we wrap, let's be clear on who's gonna do what.

english: With all these things in mind / With all that being said
meaning: considering everything discussed
example: With all that being said, I'd recommend we move forward.

meaning: after acknowledging all the above
example: With that being said, here's my recommendation.

meaning: to solve or figure out collaboratively
example: We don't have the answer yet — let's work that out together.

meaning: expressing a hypothetical past outcome
example: That would have been the right call a year ago.

english: Would you be able to do [X]?
meaning: a polite request
example: Would you be able to do a quick review before Friday?

meaning: expressing casual agreement
example: Yeah for sure — let's include that.

meaning: roughly accurate; essentially yes
example: Is this what you were going for?" — "Yeah, pretty much.

meaning: strong, enthusiastic agreement
example: We should lead with the human story." — "Yeah, 100%.

meaning: a very long span of time
example: This has been a challenge for years and years and years.

english: Yes, I was going to mention that
meaning: confirming you had the same thought
example: Yes, I was going to mention that — the timing is also an issue.

english: You can make it your daily habit
meaning: something that can be incorporated into a regular routine

english: You can't break the rules unless you know the rules
meaning: mastery of fundamentals is required before subverting them

english: You done it actually when you first came back
meaning: acknowledging something someone did in a past situation

english: You gotta keep moving forward / never step back
meaning: the importance of persistence and forward momentum
you need to persist despite difficulty
example: It'll feel hard at first, but you gotta push through it.

english: You had quite a year that included…
meaning: acknowledging someone's eventful or significant period
checking for understanding or agreement
example: It's more of a feeling than a formula — you know what I'm saying?

english: You know, very much a collaborative effort
meaning: crediting teamwork for a result
introducing an ironic or amusing observation
example: You know, what's funny is, we almost went a completely different direction.

meaning: suggesting something might not be necessary or expected
example: You might not even need a second round of edits.

meaning: acknowledging multiple qualities
example: You not only delivered on time, but also exceeded the brief.

meaning: enthusiastically recommending someone see something
example: You should see the new office — it's incredible.

meaning: there are remaining obstacles
example: You still have some barrier to entry — the price point is too high.
